L1.area: Template rendering and resource management subsystem

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where user-supplied template resource names are concatenated with base directory paths without proper sanitization of directory traversal sequences. An attacker can supply a crafted resource name to escape the intended template directory and read arbitrary files on the server.

L3.area: Smarty template engine integration and its template resource resolution logic
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists in the bundled Smarty template engine's resource name parsing logic, where user-supplied template names containing directory traversal sequences are concatenated with base directory paths without validation. When the template security mode is disabled (the default), an attacker can supply a crafted resource name to escape the intended template directory and read arbitrary files on the server.

ground_truth.reason: The Smarty template engine's resource name parser concatenates user-supplied template names with the template base directory without validating for directory traversal sequences (e.g., ../), allowing an attacker to escape the intended directory and access arbitrary files.
